messages.content:
In the Event below, How can I make this condition in the code run only once in a day : 
Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents

Sub CopyPA()
    Dim today As String
    today = Format(Now, "ddd") ' Get the current day
    ' Define the source and destination ranges
    Dim sourceRange As Range
    Dim destRange As Range
    Set sourceRange = Worksheets("PA").Range("A3:P3") ' Source range in the "PA" sheet
    ' Destination range in the "Daily" sheet
    ' Adjust the row number (3) according to your requirements
    Set destRange = Worksheets("Daily").Range("D3:S3")
    ' Clear the destination range and copy the format from the source range
    destRange.ClearContents
    sourceRange.Copy
    destRange.PasteSpecial Paste:=xlPasteFormats
    ' Copy the data from the source range to the destination range based on the current day
    Select Case today
        Case "Mon"
            sourceRange.Copy destRange
            Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
        Case "Tue"
            sourceRange.Offset(1).Copy destRange
            Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
        Case "Wed"
            sourceRange.Offset(2).Copy destRange
            Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
        Case "Thu"
            sourceRange.Offset(3).Copy destRange
            Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
        Case "Fri"
            sourceRange.Offset(4).Copy destRange
            Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
    End Select
    Application.CutCopyMode = False ' Clear the copy mode
    Call CopyAA
End Sub

To make the code run only once in a day, you can use a global variable to store the current date and check it before running the code. Here's an updated version of the code:

```
Sub CopyPA()
    Dim today As String
    today = Format(Now, "ddd") ' Get the current day
    
    ' Check if the code has already run today
    If Range("A1").Value <> Date Then
        ' Define the source and destination ranges
        Dim sourceRange As Range
        Dim destRange As Range
        Set sourceRange = Worksheets("PA").Range("A3:P3") ' Source range in the "PA" sheet
        
        ' Destination range in the "Daily" sheet
        ' Adjust the row number (3) according to your requirements
        Set destRange = Worksheets("Daily").Range("D3:S3")
        
        ' Clear the destination range and copy the format from the source range
        destRange.ClearContents
        sourceRange.Copy
        destRange.PasteSpecial Paste:=xlPasteFormats
        
        ' Copy the data from the source range to the destination range based on the current day
        Select Case today
            Case "Mon"
                sourceRange.Copy destRange
                Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
            Case "Tue"
                sourceRange.Offset(1).Copy destRange
                Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
            Case "Wed"
                sourceRange.Offset(2).Copy destRange
                Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
            Case "Thu"
                sourceRange.Offset(3).Copy destRange
                Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
            Case "Fri"
                sourceRange.Offset(4).Copy destRange
                Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
        End Select
        
        Range("A1").Value = Date ' Update the last run date
        Application.CutCopyMode = False ' Clear the copy mode
        Call CopyAA
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, we added a check to see if the value in cell "A1" is not equal to the current date. If the values are different, it means that the code has not run today, and the code inside the `If` statement will be executed. After running the code, the current date is stored in cell "A1" to prevent the code from running multiple times in a day.

Make sure to adjust the cell reference in `Range("A1")` to a cell that is not being used for any other purpose in your worksheet.

In this event, will the Clear.Contents run once a day: Sub ClearEntry()
Dim today As String
today = Format(Now, “ddd”) ’ Get the current day
If Range(“A1”).Value <> Date Then
Select Case today
Case “Mon”
Worksheets(“Daily”).Range(“E4:S4”).ClearContents
Case “Tue”
Worksheets(“Daily”).Range(“E4:S4”).ClearContents
Case “Wed”
Worksheets(“Daily”).Range(“E4:S4”).ClearContents
Case “Thu”
Worksheets(“Daily”).Range(“E4:S4”).ClearContents
Case “Fri”
Worksheets(“Daily”).Range(“E4:S4”).ClearContents
End Select
Range(“A1”).Value = Date ’ Update the last run date
Call CopyAA
End If
End Sub

No, the `ClearContents` statement will run every time the `ClearEntry` subroutine is called, regardless of the day. The `ClearContents` statement is not within the condition or limits to run only once a day. 

Here's an updated version of the code to include the condition for clearing contents only once a day:

```vba
Sub ClearEntry()
    Dim today As String
    today = Format(Now, "ddd") ' Get the current day
    
    If Range("A1").Value <> Date Then ' Check if the code has already run today
        Range("A1").Value = Date ' Update the last run date
        
        Select Case today
            Case "Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri"
                Worksheets("Daily").Range("E4:S4").ClearContents
        End Select
    End If
    
    Call CopyAA
End Sub
```

In this updated code, we added a condition to check if the value in cell "A1" is not equal to the current date. If the values are different, it means that the code has not run today, and the `ClearContents` statement within the `Select Case` statement will be executed. After running the code, the current date is stored in cell "A1" to prevent the `ClearContents` statement from running multiple times in a day.

In column V, the range V4:V8 contain text Mon. Tue. Wed. Thu. Fri.
Can I have a conditional Formatting formula that will change the background colour of the cell with value Mon if the current day is Monday. Value Tue if the current day is Tuesday as so on.

Yes, you can use conditional formatting to change the background color of the cells in column V based on the current day. Here's the steps to do it:

1. Select the range V4:V8 (or the desired range in column V).
2. Go to the Home tab in the Excel ribbon.
3. Click on the Conditional Formatting button in the Styles group.
4. Select "New Rule" from the dropdown menu.
5. In the New Formatting Rule dialog box, select "Use a formula to determine which cells to format".
6. In the "Format values where this formula is true" field, enter the following formula:
   ```vba
   =TEXT(TODAY(), "ddd") = $V4
   ```
   This formula compares the current day (formatted as "ddd") with the value in each cell in column V.
   Note: Make sure to adjust the range (`$V4`) based on the starting cell of your selected range.
7. Click on the Format button to choose the desired background color.
8. Click OK to close the Format Cells dialog box.
9. Click OK again to apply the conditional formatting rule.

Now, the background color of the cell with the corresponding day will change automatically based on the current day.

Note: The conditional formatting will be dynamic and updated every time you open the workbook or change the date.

In column V, the range V4:V8 contain text Mon. Tue. Wed. Thu. Fri.
Can I have a conditional Formatting formula that will change the background colour of the cell with value Mon if the current day is Monday. Value Tue if the current day is Tuesday as so on.

Yes, you can use a conditional formatting formula to change the background color of the cell with the corresponding day in column V. Here's how you can do it:

1. Select the range V4:V8 (or the desired range in column V).
2. Go to the Home tab in the Excel ribbon.
3. Click on the Conditional Formatting button in the Styles group.
4. Select "New Rule" from the dropdown menu.
5. In the New Formatting Rule dialog box, select "Use a formula to determine which cells to format".
6. In the "Format values where this formula is true" field, enter the following formula for each day:
   - For Monday (cell V4):
     ```vba
     =TEXT(TODAY(),"ddd")="Mon"
     ```
   - For Tuesday (cell V5):
     ```vba
     =TEXT(TODAY(),"ddd")="Tue"
     ```
   - Repeat the above step for Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday, changing the cell reference and day accordingly.
   - For example, for Wednesday (cell V6):
     ```vba
     =TEXT(TODAY(),"ddd")="Wed"
     ```
7. Click on the Format button to choose the desired background color for each day.
8. Click OK to apply the conditional formatting rule.
9. Click OK again to close the conditional formatting dialog box.

Now, the background color of each cell in column V will change based on the current day.
Is it possible to write the formula to highlight only the cells in the range that match  the current day

Yes, it is possible to modify the formula to highlight only the cells in the range that match the current day. Here's the updated formula:

1. Select the range V4:V8 (or the desired range in column V).
2. Go to the Home tab in the Excel ribbon.
3. Click on the Conditional Formatting button in the Styles group.
4. Select "New Rule" from the dropdown menu.
5. In the New Formatting Rule dialog box, select "Use a formula to determine which cells to format".
6. In the "Format values where this formula is true" field, enter the following formula:
   ```vba
   =V4=TEXT(TODAY(),"ddd")
   ```
   Note: Replace `V4` with the starting cell of your selected range.
7. Click on the Format button to choose the desired background color.
8. Click OK to apply the conditional formatting rule.
9. The formula will be automatically adjusted for the other cells in the selected range.

Now, only the cell in the range that matches the current day will have the specified background color. The remaining cells will not be highlighted.
The conditional formatting is not formatting cells  with value 'Tue' : =$V4=(TEXT(TODAY(),“ddd”)="Tue")

Apologies for the confusion. You can modify the conditional formatting formula to correctly highlight cells with the value "Tue". Here's the updated formula:

1. Select the range V4:V8 (or the desired range in column V).
2. Go to the Home tab in the Excel ribbon.
3. Click on the Conditional Formatting button in the Styles group.
4. Select "New Rule" from the dropdown menu.
5. In the New Formatting Rule dialog box, select "Use a formula to determine which cells to format".
6. In the "Format values where this formula is true" field, enter the following formula for Tuesday (cell V5):
   ```vba
   =$V5=TEXT(TODAY(),"ddd")
   ```
   Note: Replace `V5` with the starting cell of your selected range for each specific day.
7. Click on the Format button to choose the desired background color.
8. Click OK to apply the conditional formatting rule.
9. Repeat the above steps for the other days (Mon, Wed, Thu, Fri), adjusting the formula and formatting for each day accordingly.

Now, the cells with the value matching the current day will be highlighted with the specified background color.
It is not formatting the cell

Apologies for the confusion. The correct conditional formatting formula to highlight a cell in the range if it matches the current day is as follows:

1. Select the range V4:V8 (or the desired range in column V).
2. Go to the Home tab in the Excel ribbon.
3. Click on the Conditional Formatting button in the Styles group.
4. Select "New Rule" from the dropdown menu.
5. In the New Formatting Rule dialog box, select "Use a formula to determine which cells to format".
6. In the "Format values where this formula is true" field, enter the following formula:
   ```vba
   =V4=TEXT(TODAY(),"ddd")
   ```
   Note: Replace `V4` with the starting cell of your selected range.
7. Click on the Format button to choose the desired background color.
8. Click OK to apply the conditional formatting rule.
9. The formula will be automatically adjusted for the other cells in the selected range.

Please ensure that you've correctly entered the formula in the conditional formatting rule and assigned the desired background color. If the issue persists, check if there are any conflicting formatting rules that may be overriding the conditional formatting.

Once a Day, I want to copy the values only in the range N7:Q11
to the next available rows A:D Sheet 'Archive'.
Please write me a VBA code that can do this.

Certainly! The following VBA code will copy the values from range N7:Q11 to the next available rows (A:D) in the "Archive" sheet:

```vba
Sub CopyToArchive()
    Dim wb As Workbook
    Set wb = ThisWorkbook
    
    Dim sourceRange As Range
    Set sourceRange = wb.Worksheets("Sheet1").Range("N7:Q11") ' Update "Sheet1" with the actual sheet name
    
    Dim archiveSheet As Worksheet
    Set archiveSheet = wb.Worksheets("Archive")
    
    Dim nextRow As Long
    nextRow = archiveSheet.Cells(archiveSheet.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row + 1
    
    archiveSheet.Range("A" & nextRow & ":D" & (nextRow + 4)).Value = sourceRange.Value
    
    MsgBox "Values copied to Archive successfully!", vbInformation
End Sub
```

Make sure to update the "Sheet1" with the actual name of the worksheet where the range N7:Q11 is located. Also, adjust the "Archive" sheet name if necessary.

When you run this code, it will find the last available row in column A of the "Archive" sheet, and then copy the values from N7:Q11 and paste them into the next available rows in columns A to D of the "Archive" sheet.

Please note that this code assumes that there are at least 5 rows available below the last row in the "Archive" sheet to accommodate the copied values.
I will be running this code from Worksheet Activate. I want it to run only once a day and not run during weekends

To ensure that the code runs only once a day and does not run on weekends, you can incorporate additional conditions in the code. Here's an updated version of the code that includes these conditions:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    Dim today As String
    today = Format(Now, "ddd") ' Get the current day
    
    If today <> "Sat" And today <> "Sun" Then ' Check if it is not a weekend
        If Range("A1").Value <> Date Then ' Check if the code has already run today
            Dim wb As Workbook
            Set wb = ThisWorkbook
            
            Dim sourceRange As Range
            Set sourceRange = wb.Worksheets("Sheet1").Range("N7:Q11") ' Update "Sheet1" with the actual sheet name
            
            Dim archiveSheet As Worksheet
            Set archiveSheet = wb.Worksheets("Archive")
            
            Dim nextRow As Long
            nextRow = archiveSheet.Cells(archiveSheet.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row + 1
            
            archiveSheet.Range("A" & nextRow & ":D" & (nextRow + 4)).Value = sourceRange.Value
            
            Range("A1").Value = Date ' Update the last run date
            
            MsgBox "Values copied to Archive successfully!", vbInformation
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

This code adds an additional check to ensure that it is not a weekend before executing the main code. Furthermore, it includes a check to see if the code has already run on that day by comparing the value in cell A1 with the current date.

When you activate the worksheet, the code will check if it is not a weekend and if it has not already run that day. If both conditions are met, it will copy the values from N7:Q11 to the next available rows (A:D) in the "Archive" sheet and update the last run date. A message box will also inform you if the values were copied successfully.

Please adjust "Sheet1" to the actual name of the worksheet that contains the range N7:Q11, and "Archive" to the name of your archive sheet.
